questions.text: What was the initial reaction of the group of Jinn when they heard the Quran?
questions.answer: They said: 'Indeed, we have heard an amazing Quran' (Qur'anan 'Ajaba).

questions.text: To what does the Quran guide according to the Jinn?
questions.answer: It guides to the right course (ar-Rushd).

questions.text: What did the Jinn realize about their previous beliefs regarding Allah having a spouse or a son?
questions.answer: They realized it was an 'exorbitant lie' against Allah.

questions.text: Did the Jinn previously think that humans and jinn would not lie about Allah?
questions.answer: Yes, they assumed no one would ever speak a lie against Allah.

questions.text: How did some humans increase the Jinn in 'rebellion' or 'burden'?
questions.answer: By seeking refuge in men of the jinn (a form of shirk/superstition).

questions.text: What did the Jinn find when they tried to reach the heaven to eavesdrop?
questions.answer: They found it filled with powerful guards and burning flames (missiles).

questions.text: Can the Jinn know the unseen (Ghaib) about the future on earth?
questions.answer: No, they admitted they do not know if evil is intended for those on earth or if their Lord intends for them a right course.

questions.text: Are all Jinn righteous?
questions.answer: No, they are of various sects; some are righteous and some are otherwise.

questions.text: What is the consequence for a Jinn (or human) who believes in his Lord?
questions.answer: He will not fear any loss or burden.

questions.text: What happens to those Jinn who are 'unjust' (the Qasitun)?
questions.answer: They will be fuel for Hellfire.

questions.text: What would Allah have given people if they remained constant on the right way?
questions.answer: Abundant water/provision as a test for them.

questions.text: To whom do the Masajid (places of worship) belong?
questions.answer: To Allah alone; so do not call upon anyone with Allah.

questions.text: What was the reaction of the people when the servant of Allah (the Prophet) stood up to pray?
questions.answer: They almost crowded over him in opposition.

questions.text: Can the Prophet (SAW) cause harm or bring right guidance to anyone by himself?
questions.answer: No, he possesses no power to cause harm or right guidance; it is only from Allah.

questions.text: What is the only protection the Prophet (SAW) has?
questions.answer: Allah; he can find no refuge besides Him.

questions.text: Who knows the unseen entirely?
questions.answer: Allah, the Knower of the unseen.

questions.text: Under what condition does Allah reveal part of the unseen to a messenger?
questions.answer: If He chooses that messenger, and He sends before and behind him guards (angels) to protect the revelation.

questions.text: Does Allah keep count of all things?
questions.answer: Yes, He has encompassed all they have and has enumerated all things in number.

questions.text: True or False: The Jinn mentioned in this Surah became Muslims and went back to warn their people.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: What is the fate of the one who is turned away from the reminder of his Lord?
questions.answer: Allah will thrust him into a severe punishment.
